transformation_type: Z-selective phosphonate olefination of aldehydes and ketones
mechanism_type: phosphonate carbanion addition and chelation-controlled oxaphosphetane collapse favoring Z-alkenes
common_conditions: deprotonate a bis(trifluoroethyl) phosphonate with a strong base in the presence of 18-crown-6 and condense with an aldehyde to favor Z-alkene formation
synonym_names: Still-Gennari HWE Olefination|Still-Gennari Modified HWE|Still-Gennari Olefination
description_short: Z-selective variant of the Horner-Wadsworth-Emmons olefination that employs strongly electron-withdrawing phosphonate esters and crown ether additives.

transformation_type: nitrile-based aromatic acylation to aryl ketones
mechanism_type: acid or Lewis-acid activation of nitriles to nitrilium/iminium species followed by electrophilic aromatic substitution and hydrolysis
common_conditions: activate an aliphatic or aromatic nitrile under acidic or Lewis-acidic conditions and react it with an electron-rich arene, then hydrolyze the iminium salt to the ketone
description_short: Electrophilic aromatic acylation that converts activated nitriles and electron-rich arenes into aromatic ketones after hydrolysis.

transformation_type: decarboxylative halogenation of carboxylic acid derivatives
mechanism_type: formation of acyl hypohalite followed by radical decarboxylation and halogen transfer
common_conditions: convert a carboxylic acid to a heavy-metal carboxylate or related precursor and treat with halogen or halogenating reagent to obtain the one-carbon-shorter halide
description_short: Classical decarboxylative halogenation manifold that transforms carboxylic acids or salts into shorter-chain alkyl, aryl, or vinyl halides.

transformation_type: cobalt-salen catalyzed kinetic resolution of terminal epoxides with water
mechanism_type: cooperative bimetallic epoxide activation and selective hydrolytic ring opening
common_conditions: treat a racemic terminal epoxide with substoichiometric water in the presence of a chiral Co(salen) catalyst to obtain enantioenriched epoxide and diol products
description_short: Cobalt-salen mediated hydrolytic kinetic resolution delivering enantioenriched terminal epoxides and their complementary vicinal diols.

transformation_type: asymmetric epoxidation of unfunctionalized or electron-rich alkenes
mechanism_type: Mn(salen)-mediated oxygen transfer from hypochlorite, iodosylbenzene, or peracids to alkenes
common_conditions: oxidize an alkene with a chiral Mn(salen) catalyst and NaOCl, PhIO, or peracid oxidant under carefully tuned temperature and additive conditions to obtain optically active epoxides
description_short: Chiral Mn(salen)-catalyzed asymmetric epoxidation platform for unfunctionalized and related alkene substrates.
